question:
Q.1 Identify the meaning of the idiom:
**Barking up the wrong tree**
options: Following a wrong course of action
answer: Following a wrong course of action
explanation:
• **Following a wrong course of action**: Looking in the wrong place or accusing the wrong person. (Related: Misdirected effort). 🌳
• **Being very loud**: Making a lot of noise. (Related: Vociferous). 📢

question:
Q.2 Select the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom:
He **cried wolf** so many times that no one believed him when he was actually in trouble.
explanation:
• **Raised a false alarm**: To ask for help when you don't need it, so that people ignore you when you really do. (Related: Deceptive). 🐺
• **Shouted for help**: Calling for assistance. (Related: S.O.S). 🆘

question: Q.3 What does **'Back to square one'** mean?
options: To go back to the starting point
answer: To go back to the starting point
explanation:
• **Starting point**: To start over again because a previous attempt failed. (Related: Start from scratch). ↩️
• **Reach the end**: To complete a task. (Related: Crossing the finish line). 🏁

question:
Q.4 Choose the correct meaning for the idiom:
**To burn one's fingers**
options: To get into trouble by meddling
answer: To get into trouble by meddling
explanation:
• **Trouble by meddling**: To suffer unpleasant consequences as a result of your own actions or interference. (Related: Backfire). 🔥
• **Cook carefully**: Preparing food with caution. (Antonym: Slipshod cooking). 🍳

question: Q.5 The idiom **'To cut a long story short'** is used when you want to:
explanation:
• **End quickly**: To leave out extra details and get to the main point. (Related: In a nutshell, Briefly). ✂️
• **Tell a boring story**: A long, uninteresting narrative. (Related: Rigmarole). 🥱

question: Q.6 If you **'bite off more than you can chew'**, you:
options: Take on a task that is too big
answer: Take on a task that is too big
explanation:
• **Task too big**: Trying to do something that is too difficult for you. (Related: Overextending oneself). 🦷
• **Eat too fast**: Consuming food rapidly. (Related: Gulping). 🍔

question: Q.7 **To let the cat out of the bag** means:
options: To reveal a secret by mistake
answer: To reveal a secret by mistake
explanation:
• **Reveal a secret**: To accidentally or carelessly disclose confidential information. (Related: Spill the beans). 🐈
• **Tell a lie**: To say something that is not true. (Related: Fib). 🤥

question: Q.8 Someone who **'blows their own trumpet'**:
explanation:
• **Self-praise**: To talk boastfully about one's own talents or successes. (Related: Bragging, Boasting). 🎺
• **Professional musician**: Someone whose job is to play music. (Related: Virtuoso). 🎵

question: Q.9 A **'storm in a teacup'** refers to:
options: Useless anger over a trivial matter
answer: Useless anger over a trivial matter
explanation:
• **Trivial matter**: Great excitement or anger about something unimportant. (Related: Much ado about nothing). ☕
• **Major disaster**: An event causing great damage. (Related: Catastrophe). 🌪️

question: Q.10 To **'leave no stone unturned'** means:
explanation:
• **Possible effort**: To do everything possible to find something or solve a problem. (Related: Thoroughly, Exhaustively). 🪨
• **Build a wall**: To construct a barrier. (Related: Fortify). 🧱

question: Q.11 When you **'eat humble pie'**, you:
options: Admit you were wrong and apologize
answer: Admit you were wrong and apologize
explanation:
• **Apologize**: To admit that you were wrong and accept humiliation. (Related: Eat crow). 🥧
• **Behave arrogantly**: Acting in a superior or haughty manner. (Antonym: Modest). 🤨

question: Q.12 To **'face the music'** means:
explanation:
• **Accept consequences**: To deal with the unpleasant results of your own actions. (Related: Pay the piper). 🎶
• **Ignore criticism**: To pay no attention to negative feedback. (Antonym). 🙉

question: Q.13 If a situation **'gets out of hand'**, it:
explanation:
• **Difficult to control**: A situation that is no longer under management or restraint. (Related: Unruly, Chaotic). ✋
• **Easy to manage**: Simple to handle. (Antonym: A breeze). ✅

question: Q.14 **To hit the sack** means:
explanation:
• **Go to bed**: To go to sleep. (Related: Hit the hay). 😴
• **Lose a job**: Being fired or dismissed. (Related: Get the sack). 💼

question: Q.15 **To keep at arm's length** means:
explanation:
• **Avoid friendliness**: To avoid being too close or involved with someone. (Related: Keep one's distance). 📏
• **Hug someone**: To hold someone tightly. (Antonym: Embrace). 🤗

question: Q.16 When you **'jump on the bandwagon'**, you:
explanation:
• **Popular trend**: To join others in doing something that has become fashionable or successful. (Related: Follow the crowd). 🚂
• **Avoid competition**: Staying away from rivalry. (Related: Monopoly). 🛑

question: Q.17 **To keep the wolf from the door** means:
explanation:
• **Avoid poverty**: To have enough money to buy basic food and avoid starving. (Related: Eke out a living). 🐺
• **Guard a house**: Protecting property. (Related: Sentry). 🏠

question: Q.18 **To make both ends meet** means:
options: To earn just enough money to survive
answer: To earn just enough money to survive
explanation:
• **Just enough money**: To have enough money to pay for the basic things you need. (Related: Living from hand to mouth). 💵
• **Finish a task**: To complete an assignment. (Related: Wrap up). ✅

question: Q.19 To **'play second fiddle'** means:
explanation:
• **Subordinate role**: To be in a less important position than someone else. (Related: Underling). 🎻
• **Be the leader**: To be in charge. (Antonym: To lead the pack). 👑

question: Q.20 If you **'smell a rat'**, you:
options: Have a good sense of smell
Suspect something is wrong or dishonest
answer: Suspect something is wrong or dishonest
explanation:
• **Suspect dishonesty**: To believe that something wrong or a deception is happening. (Related: Fishy). 🐀
• **Dirty place**: A location that is not clean. (Related: Squalid). 🧹
